entities.name: CHICKPEA
entities.description: The primary crop being studied for resistance to the beet armyworm

entities.name: BEET ARMYWORM
entities.description: The pest Spodoptera exigua that attacks several economically important crops including chickpea

entities.name: SOUTH CENTRAL INDIA
entities.description: The region where the beet armyworm recently emerged as a serious pest of chickpea

entities.name: DETACHED LEAF ASSAY
entities.description: A standardized technique used to evaluate chickpea germplasm and segregating populations for resistance to the beet armyworm

entities.name: RESISTANCE
entities.description: The ability of chickpea genotypes to withstand infestation by the beet armyworm

entities.name: LABORATORY CONDITIONS
entities.description: The controlled environment where the detached leaf assay was performed

entities.name: ICCL 86111
entities.description: A chickpea genotype that showed higher resistance to the beet armyworm

entities.name: ICC 3137
entities.description: A chickpea genotype that showed lower resistance to the beet armyworm

entities.name: FIELD CONDITIONS
entities.description: One of the environments where the test genotypes were grown

entities.name: GREENHOUSE CONDITIONS
entities.description: One of the environments where the test genotypes were grown

entities.name: VEGETATIVE STAGE
entities.description: A growth stage of the chickpea plant used during the detached leaf assay

entities.name: FLOWERING STAGE
entities.description: A growth stage of the chickpea plant used during the detached leaf assay

entities.name: PODDING STAGE
entities.description: A growth stage of the chickpea plant used during the detached leaf assay

entities.name: NEONATE LARVAE
entities.description: The early larval stage of the beet armyworm used for infestation in the assay

entities.name: THIRD-INSTAR LARVAE
entities.description: The later larval stage of the beet armyworm used for infestation in the assay

entities.name: LARVAL SURVIVAL
entities.description: The rate of survival of the beet armyworm larvae on different chickpea genotypes, used as a measure of resistance
